mission.title: argo-rollouts: Traffic management for kong ingress controller and kuma
mission.description: Traffic management for kong ingress controller and kuma. Requested by 47+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-rollouts deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-rollouts version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
helm list -n argo-rollouts 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-rollouts installation.

steps.title: Review argo-rollouts configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-rollouts configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get configmap -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-rollouts
```
Currently I see that only the below are supported
* Istio
* Nginx Ingress Controller
* AWS ALB Ingress Controller
* Service Mesh Interface (SMI)

steps.title: Apply the fix for Traffic management for kong ingress controller and kuma
steps.description:
,  ,  ,  

Full example with Kong via the Gateway API can be found here https://github.com/argoproj-labs/rollouts-plugin-trafficrouter-gatewayapi/tree/main/examples/kong

Any feedback welcome
```yaml
minikube addons disable ingress
helm install kong kong/kong --set proxy.type=NodePort
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get events -n argo-rollouts --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Traffic management for kong ingress controller and kuma" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
,  ,  ,  

Full example with Kong via the Gateway API can be found here https://github.com/argoproj-labs/rollouts-plugin-trafficrouter-gatewayapi/tree/main/examples/kong

Any feedback welcome
codeSnippets:

helm install kong kong/kong --set proxy.type=NodePort

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-rollouts installed or the issue environment reproducible.
